mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity risk premiums and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions that precede corporate revenue contractions, causing immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal fails to predict returns with a t-statistic exceeding 1.96.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous industrial production expansion and housing permit contraction indicate a sectoral rotation from rate-sensitive construction to manufacturing and tech, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns as earnings quality improves.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative predictive R-squared over a 3-year out-of-sample test, or SPX forward returns do not differ significantly between high/low INDPRO and high/low HOUST quartiles.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate margins and forces prolonged restrictive monetary policy, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter when inflation exceeds the Federal Reserve's target band.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or neutral when CPIAUCSL accelerates above the 3.5% annualized threshold, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% in backtesting.

mechanism_narrative: Growth in retail sales and food services revenue reflects robust aggregate consumer demand, which directly supports corporate top-line growth and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: The rolling 1-year Spearman correlation between RSAFS month-over-month changes and SPX 5-day forward returns falls below 0.05, indicating no persistent predictive relationship.

mechanism_narrative: A breakdown in industrial output below the long-term trend indicates supply chain stress or demand destruction that will compress profit margins, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly negative when INDPRO breaches the 100 threshold, or the signal generates more false positives than true negatives over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent payroll growth and unemployment rate declines confirm a self-reinforcing labor demand expansion that sustains consumer spending power, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term in a multivariate regression yields a coefficient with a p-value greater than 0.10, or forward returns do not exhibit a monotonic relationship with the joint labor signal.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth following a recessionary trough signals the onset of a new business cycle expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent week as earnings revisions turn sharply positive.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain flat or negative when GDPC1 quarterly growth exceeds 2.5% annualized, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark over three consecutive recovery episodes.
